Philosophy

The Gaudiya Vaishnava Path

Bhakti is not a sentiment; it is a science of the soul.

Navadha Bhakti: The Nine Processes

In the Srimad Bhagavatam (7.5.23), Prahlada Maharaja explains the nine primary ways to serve the Lord:

  • Sravanam: Hearing about the Lord (the root of all bhakti).
  • Kirtanam: Chanting His glories (the yuga-dharma).
  • Smaranam: Remembering His form and pastimes.
  • Pada-sevanam: Serving the lotus feet of the Lord.
  • Archanam: Worshipping the Deity in the temple.
  • Vandanam: Offering prayers of submission.
  • Dasyam: Serving as a dedicated servant.
  • Sakhyam: Cultivating a friendship with the Lord.
  • Atma-nivedanam: Full surrender of the self.

Achintya Bheda Abheda

Lord Chaitanya taught the philosophy of "Inconceivable simultaneous oneness and difference." This means the living entity is qualitatively one with God—being sat-chit-ananda—but quantitatively different, just like a golden ornament is gold but not the entire gold mine.

The Power of the Holy Name

Names like 'Krishna' and 'Rama' are not material labels. They are potent spiritual frequencies. By chanting correctly, one's consciousness is elevated from the platform of body and mind to the platform of pure spirit soul (atma).

"harer nama harer nama harer namaiva kevalam
kalau nasty eva nasty eva nasty eva gatir anyatha"

In this age of quarrel and hypocrisy, the only means of deliverance is chanting the holy name of the Lord. There is no other way. There is no other way. There is no other way.

Brihan-naradiya Purana

Kirtan Premi

Propagating the sound incarnation of God through the legacy of Gaudiya Vaishnavism.
